name: Aegon Targaryen (the Uncrowned)
disambiguator: Son of Aenys; Prince of Dragonstone; "Aegon the Uncrowned"; sister-husband of Rhaena; rode Quicksilver

name: Rhaena Targaryen
disambiguator: Daughter of Aenys; sister-wife of Aegon the Uncrowned; mother of Aerea & Rhaella; rode Dreamfyre; a Black Bride

name: Visenya Targaryen
disambiguator: Dowager Queen; mother of Maegor

name: Ser Morgan Hightower
disambiguator: Received the only full royal pardon; late of the Warrior's Sons; first victim of the Red Dog

name: Ser Tyler Hill
disambiguator: Bastard son of Lyman Lannister; led 500 Lannisport men for Aegon

name: Lord Corbray
disambiguator: Of the Vale; slew Davos Darklyn with Lady Forlorn; advised Aegon to fight foes separately

name: Lord Tully
disambiguator: Of Riverrun; fought for Maegor; later hosted Joffrey Doggett as a guest

name: Ser Davos Darklyn
disambiguator: Of the Kingsguard; marshalled 5,000 swords; slain by Lord Corbray

name: Lord Peake
disambiguator: Reach lord for Maegor; his son and heir later slain by Warrior's Sons

name: Lord Caswell
disambiguator: Reach lord who marched for Maegor

name: Ser Willam Whistler
disambiguator: Died with Aegon at the Gods Eye

name: Ser Walton Towers
disambiguator: Won the Harrenhal melee; named Lord of Harrenhal; died of wounds within a fortnight

name: Lord Alton Butterwell
disambiguator: Master of coin; granted Lord Harroway's Town; offered his widowed sister as a wife

name: Lord Darnold Darry
disambiguator: Granted the rest of the Harroway holdings

name: Ser Owen Bush
disambiguator: Kingsguard; rumored to have killed Ceryse; found dead outside a brothel

name: Lord Edwell Celtigar
disambiguator: New Hand of the King; had two maiden daughters; later resigned to Claw Isle

name: Ser Joffrey Doggett
disambiguator: The Red Dog of the Hills; self-proclaimed Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons

name: Ser Horys Hill
disambiguator: Bastard hedge knight who escaped the Great Fork; led the Wendwater rescue and died

name: Grand Maester Benifer
disambiguator: Suggested Clarisse Dayne as a bride; fled to Pentos

name: Lord Velaryon
disambiguator: Lord of Driftmark; advised Maegor to wed Rhaena; among first to declare for Jaehaerys

name: Ser Maladon Moore
disambiguator: Kingsguard; helped seize Tyanna

name: Ser Theo Bolling
disambiguator: Landed knight; first husband of Elinor Costayne; beheaded for alleged conspiracy

name: Lord Rogar Baratheon
disambiguator: Of Storm's End; proclaimed Jaehaerys king; named Protector of the Realm and Hand

name: Lord Oakheart
disambiguator: Appeared with levies and joined Septon Moon

name: Lord Rowan
disambiguator: Appeared with levies and joined Septon Moon

name: Lord Hayford
disambiguator: Urged Maegor to abdicate; beheaded on the spot

name: Lord Darklyn
disambiguator: Of Duskendale; among the lesser lords gathered at Maegor's end

name: Lord Massey
disambiguator: Of Stonedance; among the lesser lords at Maegor's end

name: Lord Towers
disambiguator: Of Harrenhal (Walton's heir); last with Maegor before his death

name: Lord Staunton
disambiguator: Of Rook's Rest; among the lesser lords at Maegor's end

name: Lord Bar Emmon
disambiguator: Of Sharp Point; among the lesser lords at Maegor's end

name: Lord Buckwell
disambiguator: Of the Antlers; among the lesser lords at Maegor's end

name: Balerion
disambiguator: The Black Dread; Maegor's mount; slew Quicksilver

name: Warrior's Sons
disambiguator: Military order dissolved by the High Septon; some endure under the Red Dog

name: Poor Fellows
disambiguator: Military order dissolved; hunted for bounty; some crown Septon Moon

name: Faith Militant
disambiguator: Collective name for the outlawed military orders

name: Most Devout
disambiguator: Did not demur at the bounties

name: Kingsguard
disambiguator: Maegor's sworn knights

name: Maegor I Targaryen
raw: NEEDS_VOCAB: disinherited his lawful heir by decree

name: Proclamation of Jaehaerys at Storm's End
